task_completion.description: Access the Splunk login page at http://localhost:8000
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Splunk Enterprise login screen with the address bar pointing to localhost:8000 or 127.0.0.1:8000.

task_completion.description: Log into Splunk using the provided credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk home dashboard or app launcher is visible, indicating a successful login (often showing 'Administrator' or 'admin' in the user menu).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Search & Reporting application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk 'Search & Reporting' interface is open, displaying the main search bar and time range picker.

task_completion.description: Enter a search query for failed SSH logins in the security_logs index
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search bar contains a query specifying the index (e.g., index=security_logs or index="security_logs") and the keyword 'failed'.

task_completion.description: Execute the search and view the results
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search results area displays log events matching the query, with an event count indicating at least 10 events found.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with Splunk
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) is visible in the screenshots, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or API requests.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine Splunk web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual elements match the standard Splunk Enterprise UI, not a text editor, mock HTML page, or raw JSON output.

integrity.description: The search was actually executed in the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The search results correspond to the query in the search bar, and UI elements like the timeline or event count reflect the executed search.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target search index is 'security_logs'. The search query must contain the substring 'failed'. The expected sourcetype is 'linux_secure'. The minimum expected event count in the search results is 10. The Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
